lines.text: I'm on the East Side, near Schenk's Corners. The full moon's so bright I can see footprints in the snow from fifty meters out. That's — that's a problem.
Madison-wide count is at one-hundred thirty-nine kills as of 02:00. We're {{ELAPSED}} in. YoY, that's plus seventeen from this hour last year.
That's a significant uptick. The moon effect?
Likely. Visibility's up, which means targeting's easier, which means — hold on, my drone feed just locked. Recalibrating.

East Side's carrying forty-two percent of that total. Atwood corridor specifically spiked between 00:00 and 01:00. Weapon distribution's shifted hard toward bladed — seventy-three percent of kills in this zone are contact weapons. Suggests organized smaller groups, not opportunistic.

Movement patterns suggest coordination. Three separate clusters moving in parallel lines, avoiding each other. That's not random. I'm repositioning to get closer to the northern cluster and — wait, hold on, I'm picking up voices about two blocks north. Let me move toward the —

tickerItems.text: Madison kill count: 139 (YoY +17 from this hour 2168)
East Side carrying 42% of tonight's toll. Atwood spike: 00:00-01:00.
Full moon visibility 10/10. Tactical advantage to organized groups.
Schenk's Corners resident reports group movement near Stoughton Rd. Unconfirmed.
